# B2B SaaS Buying Synthesis

## Core position

B2B SaaS buyers increasingly self-educate before sales involvement. The website, docs, reviews, AI-search footprint, demos, pricing page, and comparison pages are now part of the sales team.

## What to assume

- Buyers form shortlists before talking to sales.
- Buyers consult peers, review sites, Google, AI search, vendor sites, and product experiences.
- Buyers want transparent pricing and fast time-to-value.
- Buyers distrust unsupported vendor claims.
- Buyers need material they can carry internally to other stakeholders.

## The SaaS proof stack

| Layer | Buyer question | Asset |
|---|---|---|
| Problem | Do they understand my situation? | Use-case page, pain article, diagnostic quiz |
| Mechanism | How does it work? | Product demo, workflow video, architecture diagram |
| Fit | Is this for my team? | Persona/use-case pages, integrations, security docs |
| Proof | Has this worked for similar teams? | Case study, review mining page, testimonial cluster |
| Risk | What can go wrong? | Migration plan, limitations page, data handling, pricing |
| Action | Can I test without heavy commitment? | Trial, sandbox, interactive demo, demo booking |

## Agent instruction

For any B2B SaaS ad campaign, generate four assets, not one:

1. Ad variants.
2. Landing page proof path.
3. Internal champion one-pager.
4. Follow-up email or retargeting sequence.

## Minimum campaign file contract

```markdown
# Campaign: [name]

## Buyer state
## Stakeholders
## Pain / job
## Trigger event
## Core promise
## Proof assets
## Objections
## Offer
## Landing page path
## Measurement plan
## Follow-up sequence
```
